sectiondef.header: DP generic definitions: Link bandwidth and lane count.
DP generic definitions: Bits per color components.
DP generic definitions: Miscellaneous components; color format.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field.

description.para: Address mapping for the DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) of the downstream device.

briefdescription.para: Number of receiver ports and power voltage capability.
Downstream port count, MSA timing parameters ignored, and OUI support.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX back channel symbol error count.
FAUX back channel training pattern time.
Receiver GTC value phase skew enable.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX forward channel symbol error count.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Automated testing sub-field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX back channel symbol error count control.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sink control field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sideband message buffers.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Event status indicator field.

briefdescription.para: Sink device service IRQ vector ESI 0.
Sink device service IRQ vector ESI 1.
Sink link service IRQ vector ESI 0.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Field addresses and sizes.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field masks,

briefdescription.para: No AUX handshake link training mask.
Downstream port detailed capability info available mask.
Downstream port maximum bits per component mask.
HDMI/DP++ frame sequential to frame packing mask.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field masks,

briefdescription.para: Disparity error and illegal symbol error.
MSA timing parameter ignored enable mask.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field masks, shifts,

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for sink count high/low.
Shift bits for voltage swing lane 1/3.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Field addresses and sizes.

description.para: Address mapping for the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) of the downstream device.

briefdescription.para: Size of an EDID block in bytes.
Number of extension blocks in EDID.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Register offsets for the

briefdescription.para: Pixel clock in kHz, lower 8 bits.
Pixel clock in kHz, upper 8 bits.
Horizontal resolution and blanking, upper 4 bits.
Vertical resolution and blanking, upper 4 bits.
Horizontal sync pulse width, lower 8 bits.
Vertical front porch and sync pulse width, lower 4 bits.
Horizontal and vertical front porch and sync pulse width, upper 2 bits.
Horizontal image size in mm, lower 8 bits.
Vertical image size in mm, lower 8 bits.
Horizontal and vertical image size in mm, upper 4 bits.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Masks, shifts, and register

briefdescription.para: Mask for horizontal blanking upper 4 bits.
Mask for horizontal resolution upper 4 bits.
Shift for horizontal resolution upper 4 bits.
Mask for vertical sync pulse width lower 4 bits.
Mask for vertical front porch lower 4 bits.
Shift for vertical front porch lower 4 bits.
Mask for horizontal front porch upper 2 bits.
Mask for horizontal sync pulse width upper 2 bits.
Mask for vertical front porch upper 2 bits.
Mask for vertical sync pulse width upper 2 bits.
Shift for horizontal front porch upper 2 bits.
Shift for horizontal sync pulse width upper 2 bits.
Shift for vertical front porch upper 2 bits.
Mask for vertical image size upper 4 bits.
Mask for horizontal image size upper 4 bits.
Shift for horizontal image size upper 4 bits.
This typedef enumerates the RX and TX modes of operation for the DisplayPort core.
This typedef enumerates the dynamic ranges available to the DisplayPort core.
This typedef enumerates the handlers for the the DisplayPort Receiver.
This typedef enumerates the handlers for the the DisplayPort Transmitter.
Callback type which represents a custom timer wait handler.

detaileddescription.para: This is only used for Microblaze since it doesn't have a native sleep function. To avoid dependency on a hardware timer, the default wait functionality is implemented using loop iterations; this isn't too accurate. If a custom timer handler is used, the user may implement their own wait implementation using a hardware timer (see example/) for better accuracy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of microseconds to be passed to the timer function.

briefdescription.para: Callback type which represents the handler for interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This table contains a list of global unique identifiers (GUIDs) that will be issued when exploring the topology using the algorithm in the XDp_TxFindAccessibleDpDevices function.
A table of configuration structures containing the configuration information for each DisplayPort TX core in the system.
This function waits for the DisplayPort PHY to come out of reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the PHY failed to be ready.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the configuration for this DisplayPort instance and fills in the InstancePtr->Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function prepares the DisplayPort core for use depending on whether the core is operating in TX or RX mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort core was successfully initialized.

briefdescription.para: This function installs a custom delay/sleep function to be used by the XDp driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the address to the callback function.
is the user data item (microseconds to delay) that will be passed to the custom sleep/delay function when it is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function is the delay/sleep function for the XDp driver.

detaileddescription.para: For the Zynq family, there exists native sleep functionality. For MicroBlaze however, there does not exist such functionality. In the MicroBlaze case, the default method for delaying is to use a predetermined amount of loop iterations. This method is prone to inaccuracy and dependent on system configuration; for greater accuracy, the user may supply their own delay/sleep handler, pointed to by InstancePtr->UserTimerWaitUs, which may have better accuracy if a hardware timer is used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the link rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the link rate to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified link rate is valid.
0 otherwise, if the link rate specified isn't valid as per spec, or if it exceeds the capabilities of the TX core.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the lane count.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of lanes to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified lane count is valid.
0 otherwise, if the lane count specified isn't valid as per spec, or if it exceeds the capabilities of the TX core.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XDp_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the XDp driver.

detaileddescription.para: When an interrupt happens, this interrupt handler will check which TX/RX mode of operation the core is running in, and will call the appropriate interrupt handler. The called interrupt handler will first detect what kind of interrupt happened, then decides which callback function to invoke.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the XDp driver/device depending on whether the core is operating in TX or RX mode.

detaileddescription.para: The sanity test checks whether or not all tested registers hold their default reset values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed - all tested registers hold their default reset values.

briefdescription.para: Operating in TX mode, this function will send a sideband message by creating a data array from the supplied sideband message structure and submitting an AUX write transaction.

detaileddescription.para: In RX mode, the data array will be written as a down reply.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer to the sideband message structure that holds the contents of the data to be submitted.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the write transaction used to transmit the sideband message was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no device is connected.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the request timed out.

briefdescription.para: This function will take a byte array and convert it into a sideband message format by filling in the XDp_SidebandMsg structure with the array data.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the data used to fill in the sideband message structure.
is a pointer to the sideband message structure that will be filled in with the transaction data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the transaction data was successfully converted into the sideband message structure format.
XST_FAILURE otherwise, if the calculated header or body CRC does not match that contained in the transaction data.

briefdescription.para: This function will perform a cyclic redundancy check (CRC) on the header of a sideband message using a generator polynomial of 4.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer sideband message header that the CRC algorithm is to be run on.

simplesect.para: The CRC value obtained by running the algorithm on the sideband message header.
The header is divided into 4-bit nibbles for use by the lower- level XDp_CrcCalculate function.

briefdescription.para: This function will perform a cyclic redundancy check (CRC) on the body of a sideband message using a generator polynomial of 8.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer sideband message body that the CRC algorithm is to be run on.

simplesect.para: The CRC value obtained by running the algorithm on the sideband message body.

briefdescription.para: This function will run a cyclic redundancy check (CRC) algorithm on some data given a generator polynomial.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the data that the algorithm is to run on.
is the total number of data bits that the algorithm is to run on.
is the generator polynomial for the CRC algorithm and will be used as the divisor in the polynomial long division.

simplesect.para: The CRC value obtained by running the algorithm on the data using the specified polynomial.

briefdescription.para: This is function determines whether the DisplayPort core, represented by the XDp structure pointed to, is a transmitter (TX) or a receiver (RX).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: XDP_RX if the core is of type RX. XDP_TX if the core is of type TX.

briefdescription.para: The following functions set the color encoding scheme for a given stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the stream number for which to configure the color encoding scheme for.

simplesect.para: C-style signatures: u32 XDp_TxCfgSetRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetSRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYonly(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr422Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr422Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr444Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr444Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc422Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc422Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc444Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc444Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetAdbRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream)

briefdescription.para: Contains a minimal set of functions for the XDp driver that allow access to all of the DisplayPort core's functionality.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the stream policy maker functions for the XDp driver.
This file contains static initialization methods for the XDp driver.
This file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the XDp driver.
This file contains functions related to XDp interrupt handling.
This header file contains the identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains functions related to accessing the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) of a specified sink using the XDp driver operating in TX mode.
See xdp.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Updated RX initialization with MST support. Added callbacks for lane count changes, link rate changes and pre-emphasis + voltage swing adjust requests. 5.1 als 08/09/16 Replaced deprecated MB_Sleep with consolidated usleep. 08/11/16 RX to support maximum pre-emphasis level of 1. 08/12/16 Updates to support 64-bit base addresses. 5.2 aad 01/21/17 Added training timeout disable for RX MST mode for soft-disconnect to work. 6.0 tu 05/14/17 Added AUX defer to 6

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver.

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xdp.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST registers, masks, and values for RX. Added HDCP registers and masks. 5.0 als 05/16/16 Added MISC0 and MISC1 definitions. 5.1 aad 08/16/16 Updated MISC0 definitions. 5.2 aad 01/21/17 Added timeout macro for training timeout 6.0 tu 05/14/17 Added AUX defer shift mask 6.0 tu 08/03/17 Enabled video packing for bpc > 10 6.0 tu 08/24/17 Modify #define for YCBCR422 and YCBCR444

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST interrupt handlers for RX. Guard against uninitialized callbacks. Added HDCP interrupts. Added unplug interrupt. 4.0 als 02/18/16 Removed update of payload table in the driver's interrupt handler. 6.0 tu 05/30/17 Removed unused variable in XDp_RxInterruptHandler 6.0 tu 09/08/17 Added two interrupt handler that addresses driver's internal callback function of application DrvHpdEventHandler and DrvHpdPulseHandler 6.0 tu 09/08/17 Added three interrupt handler that addresses callback function of application

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST functionality to RX. 4.0 als 02/16/16 XDp_TxAllocatePayloadStreams now allocates payloads by using an internally calculated starting timeslot for each stream rather than invoking XDp_TxGetFirstAvailableTs. XDp_TxAllocatePayloadVcIdTable now takes an additional argument (StartTs, the starting timeslot). Exposed RX MST API to application: XDp_RxAllocatePayloadStream 5.2 aad 01/24/16 XDp_RxAllocatePayloadStream now adjusts to timeslot rearragement 6.0 tu 05/30/17 Initialized variable in XDp_RxDeviceInfoToRawData

detaileddescription.para: It will check many of the DisplayPort core's register values against the default reset values as a sanity-check that the core is ready to be used.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 5.0 als 07/27/16 8B10B enable is now set by default in PHY_CONFIG reg. 6.0 tu 08/04/17 Changed Selftest regiter's default value.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 6.0 tu 02/06/17 Initialize CfgPtr to NULL.

detaileddescription.para: These functions set up the DisplayPort TX core's main stream attributes (MSA) that determine how a video stream will be displayed and also some DisplayPort RX MSA-related functions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 07/27/15 Scale TX fractional register by 1024 instead of 1000. 3.0 als 10/07/15 Added MSA callback. 4.0 als 02/07/16 Enable/disable end of line reset for reduced blanking. 5.0 als 05/16/16 Added API to set color encoding scheme. 5.1 als 08/03/16 Use video common API rather than internal structure when checking for interlaced mode. als 08/12/16 Updates to support 64-bit base addresses. 5.2 aad 01/24/17 Disable end of line reset for reduced blanking 6.0 tu 07/20/17 Allowing Custom VTM in XDp_TxCfgMsaUseStandardVideoMode function. 6.0 aad 09/05/17 Reverted to enable end of line reset for RB resolutions. 6.0 tu 09/06/17 Added Set UserPixelWidth support on tx side
